Title: TC-001: Back in Stock - No Results Display and Reset
Steps:
1. Navigate to https://vcst-qa-storefront.govirto.com/account/back-in-stock
2. Verify that the search input field is visible
3. Verify that products are listed (if any exist)
4. Enter a valid product name in the search field
5. Observe the search results
6. Clear the search field and enter "xyznonexistentproduct123"
7. Press Enter or wait for search to execute
8. Observe the page content
9. Verify the "Reset search" button is visible
10. Verify the message text matches design specs
11. Click the "Reset search" button
12. Observe the page after reset
13. Verify the search field is cleared
Expected Result:
1. Back in Stock page loads successfully
2. Search input field is displayed on the page
3. Full list of back in stock items is displayed
4. Search field accepts input
5. Products matching the search term are displayed
6. Search field accepts the invalid search term
7. Search executes and processes the query
8. "No results found" message is displayed
9. "Reset search" button is prominently displayed
10. Message text is clear and user-friendly
11. Button responds to click action
12. Full list of back in stock items is restored
13. Search field is empty after reset
Preconditions:
User is logged into VirtoStart Demo Store
User has access to the Back in Stock page
User has at least one item in the Back in Stock list

14. Observe the page after reset

4. Take screenshot of the no results page
5. Navigate to Orders page and trigger no results
6. Take screenshot of the no results page
7. Navigate to Company Members and trigger no results
8. Take screenshot of the no results page
9. Compare all screenshots side by side
10. Validate message text consistency across all pages
11. Validate button style consistency across all pages
12. Validate layout and spacing consistency
13. Open Figma design specification
14. Compare message text with design
15. Compare button style with design
16. Compare spacing/layout with design
17. Compare colors with design (use color picker)
18. Verify all typography (font, size, weight)
19. Test color contrast ratio (message text)
20. Test color contrast ratio (button)
21. Test with screen reader (NVDA/JAWS)
22. Test keyboard-only navigation
23. Test focus indicators
24. Verify ARIA labels (if applicable)
Expected Result:
1. No results page displays
2. Screenshot captured for reference
3. No results page displays
4. Screenshot captured for reference
5. No results page displays
6. Screenshot captured for reference
7. No results page displays
8. Screenshot captured for reference
9. Identify any visual inconsistencies
10. Message text is consistent or appropriately contextual
11. Button style, size, and color are identical
12. Layout and spacing are consistent
13. Design reference is accessible
14. Text matches Figma specifications
15. Button matches Figma specifications
16. Layout matches Figma specifications
17. Colors match Figma specifications
18. Typography matches Figma specifications
19. Meets WCAG AA standards (4.5:1)
20. Meets WCAG AA standards (3:1)
21. Message and button are announced properly
22. All interactive elements are accessible
23. Focus states are visible and clear
24. Proper ARIA attributes are present
Preconditions:
User is logged into VirtoStart Demo Store
All local search pages are accessible
Figma design reference is available

Title: TC-007: Cross-Browser Compatibility
Steps:
1. Test on Chrome (Windows) - Latest version
2. Navigate to each page (Back in Stock, Quotes, Orders, Members)
3. Trigger no results search on each page
4. Verify message displays correctly
5. Verify button displays correctly
6. Verify functionality works (reset button)
7. Open DevTools Console and check for errors
8. Repeat steps 1-7 on Firefox (Windows) - Latest version
9. Repeat steps 1-7 on Edge (Windows) - Latest version
10. Repeat steps 1-7 on Safari (macOS) - Latest version
11. Test on mobile Chrome (Android) - Latest version
12. Navigate to each page and trigger no results
13. Verify mobile layout is correct
14. Verify button is tappable (44px minimum)
15. Test on mobile Safari (iOS) - Latest version
16. Navigate to each page and trigger no results
17. Verify mobile layout is correct
18. Verify button is tappable
19. Document any browser-specific issues
20. Verify consistent behavior across all browsers
Expected Result:
1-19. Feature works consistently across all tested browsers
- No visual rendering differences (or only minor acceptable differences)
- No functional behavior differences
- No JavaScript errors in any browser
- Performance is acceptable in all browsers
- Touch interactions work smoothly on mobile
- Layout is responsive and functional
- Text is readable without zooming
- Buttons are easily tappable on mobile
Preconditions:
Access to multiple browsers and operating systems
User account for VirtoStart Demo Store

Title: TC-008: Responsive Design Validation
Steps:
1. Open browser DevTools (F12)
2. Enable responsive design mode
3. Test Desktop Large (1920x1080)
4. Navigate to each page and trigger no results
5. Verify message is fully visible and readable
6. Verify button is appropriately sized
7. Verify layout doesn't break
8. Test Desktop Medium (1366x768)
9. Navigate to each page and trigger no results
10. Verify all elements visible without scrolling
11. Test Tablet Landscape (1024x768)
12. Navigate to each page and trigger no results
13. Verify touch target sizes (44x44px minimum)
14. Test Tablet Portrait (768x1024)
15. Navigate to each page and trigger no results
16. Verify vertical layout works correctly
17. Test Mobile Large (414x896)
18. Navigate to each page and trigger no results
19. Verify text is readable without zoom (16px minimum)
20. Verify button is tappable (44x44px minimum)
21. Test Mobile Medium (375x667)
22. Navigate to each page and trigger no results
23. Verify all content visible within viewport
24. Test Mobile Small (320x568)
25. Navigate to each page and trigger no results
26. Verify message fits and button is accessible
27. Test orientation changes (portrait to landscape)
28. Verify layout adapts smoothly
29. Test touch interactions on mobile/tablet
30. Verify no unintended zoom or scroll behavior
Expected Result:
1-30. Layout adapts appropriately for each screen size
- No horizontal scrolling required
- All content is visible and readable
- No elements overlap or get cut off
- Proper spacing is maintained
- Buttons meet minimum touch target size (44x44px) on touch devices
- Adequate spacing between interactive elements
- Touch interactions work smoothly
- Layout adapts smoothly when rotating device
- Content remains accessible in both orientations
Preconditions:
Access to devices or browser DevTools for responsive testing
User account for VirtoStart Demo Store

Title: TC-009: Search with Valid Results
Steps:
1. Navigate to Back in Stock page
2. Note the products displayed in the list
3. Select a product name from the list
4. Enter the product name in search field
5. Execute the search
6. Observe the results
7. Verify no results message is NOT shown
8. Verify reset button is NOT shown
9. Verify search field retains the search term
10. Clear search field
11. Verify full list is restored
12. Navigate to Quotes page
13. Select a quote number from the list
14. Enter the identifier in search field
15. Execute the search and verify matching results
16. Verify no results message is NOT shown
17. Navigate to Orders page
18. Select an order number from the list
19. Enter the order number in search field
20. Execute the search and verify matching results
21. Verify no results message is NOT shown
22. Switch to "My orders" tab and repeat
23. Navigate to Company Members page
24. Select a member name from the list
25. Enter the member name in search field
26. Execute the search and verify matching results
27. Verify no results message is NOT shown
28. Test partial match searching (if supported)
29. Test case sensitivity (uppercase, lowercase, mixed)
30. Verify consistent behavior across all pages
Expected Result:
1-30. When valid search results exist:
- Matching items are displayed correctly
- Search results are relevant and accurate
- No "no results found" message appears
- No "Reset search" button appears for valid searches
- Normal search results view is maintained
- Search executes quickly (< 2 seconds)
- Search is case-insensitive (if designed that way)
- Partial matches work (if supported by design)
- Search field retains the search term
- Clearing search restores full list
- Smooth transition between full list and search results
- No page refresh required
- No errors or broken states
- Consistent behavior across all pages
Preconditions:
User is logged into VirtoStart Demo Store
User has access to all local search pages
Test data exists in the system (orders, quotes, back in stock items, members)

Title: TC-010: Edge Cases and Special Characters
Steps:
1. Navigate to Back in Stock page 2. Enter special characters: !@#$%^&*() in search field 3. Execute search and observe behavior 4. Verify no application error 5. Click reset if no results shown 6. Enter SQL injection attempt: ' OR '1'='1 7. Execute search and verify input is sanitized 8. Verify no security breach or unauthorized data access 9. Enter XSS attempt: 10. Execute search and verify script does not execute 11. Verify no alert appears (no XSS vulnerability) 12. Enter HTML tags:
test
13. Execute search and verify tags are escaped/displayed as text 14. Test empty string (press Enter without typing) 15. Verify appropriate handling (full list remains or no action) 16. Test whitespace only (spaces only) 17. Verify spaces are trimmed or handled gracefully 18. Test very long input (1000+ characters) 19. Verify input is limited or handled without crash 20. Test leading/trailing spaces: ' test ' 21. Verify spaces are trimmed appropriately 22. Test numeric values only: 123456789 23. Verify numbers are accepted and handled 24. Test mixed content: test123!@#abc 25. Verify mixed input is processed correctly 26. Test rapid input (type and delete quickly) 27. Verify UI remains responsive 28. Test copy/paste with special characters 29. Verify paste is handled safely 30. Verify no encoding issues
Expected Result:
1-30. Edge cases are handled gracefully:
- No SQL injection vulnerabilities
- No XSS vulnerabilities
- No HTML injection vulnerabilities
- Input is properly sanitized/escaped
- No unauthorized data access
- Application handles all edge cases without crashing
- No infinite loops or frozen states
- Appropriate error messages if needed
- Reset functionality works in all scenarios
- Clear feedback for invalid input (if applicable)
- UI remains functional
- Search field doesn't break or overflow
- Performance remains acceptable
- No data corruption
- No unintended data modification
Preconditions:
User is logged into VirtoStart Demo Store
User has access to all local search pages

Title: TC-011: Search with Filters - No Products Found
Steps:
1. Navigate to https://vcst-qa-storefront.govirto.com/en-GB/search
2. Verify products are displayed
3. Note the number of products displayed
4. Apply a valid filter (e.g., select a brand)
5. Verify filtered results are displayed
6. Apply additional filters that result in no matches
7. Observe the page content
8. Verify the "Reset search" button is visible
9. Verify filter selections are still visible
10. Verify the message text matches design specs
11. Click the "Reset search" button
12. Observe the page after reset
13. Verify full product catalog is restored
14. Verify filter selections are cleared
15. Test Scenario A: Apply price filter $0.01 - $0.10 (unrealistic range)
16. Verify no results message appears
17. Verify reset button is available
18. Click reset button and verify filters cleared
19. Test Scenario B: Apply multiple filters (Brand + Color + Price)
20. Combine filters that yield no matches
21. Verify no results message appears
22. Click reset button and verify all filters cleared
23. Test Scenario C: Enter search term (e.g., "laptop")
24. Apply filters that eliminate all results
25. Verify no results message appears
26. Verify search term remains visible
27. Click reset button
28. Verify filters cleared and search results restored
Expected Result:
1. Search page loads successfully
2. Product grid shows available items
3. Product count is visible
4. Products are filtered successfully
5. Only products matching filter are shown
6. Filters are applied successfully
7. "No results found" message is displayed
8. "Reset search" button is prominently displayed
9. Applied filters are shown in sidebar
10. Message text is clear and user-friendly
11. Button responds to click action
12. All filters are cleared
13. All products are displayed again
14. No filters remain selected
15-28. For all scenarios:
- Filters can be applied without errors
- No results page displays when filter combination yields no matches
- Reset button clears all applied filters
- Product catalog is restored after reset
- Filter state is reset to default
- No page refresh required (smooth transition)
- Filter counts update correctly after reset
Preconditions:
User is on VirtoStart Demo Store
User has navigated to catalog/search page with products
Multiple filters are available (price, category, brand, etc.)
